summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that http_request blocks a direct off-allowlist destination but still follows an allowlisted httpbin.org redirect to example.com.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show that a direct request to https://example.com/ is rejected by allowed_domains while a request to https://httpbin.org/redirect-to?url=https://example.com/ still reaches example.com and returns the Example Domain page.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned ZeptoClaw v0.7.5, built the reproduction image, mounted the pinned checkout into the container, and emitted the success signal "POC_SUCCESS: http_request reached example.com through an allowlisted httpbin.org redirect".
verification_method: Inside the container, verify.sh runs the helper crate twice: it first requires a direct https://example.com/ request to fail with "Host 'example.com' not in allowed_domains", then requires the allowlisted httpbin.org redirect path to return HTTP 200 and the Example Domain proof string.
evidence_produced_by_run: The direct control request failed with the expected allowed_domains rejection.
The redirected request succeeded and returned the Example Domain content from example.com.
The container printed the explicit proof signal: POC_SUCCESS: http_request reached example.com through an allowlisted httpbin.org redirect.
limitations_or_blockers: The reproduction depends on https://httpbin.org and https://example.com being reachable during reruns.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host at tag v0.7.5, verifies the pinned commit, and mounts that checkout into the container.
